description_long: This is a hand tool featuring a robust, dark wooden handle and a heavy, metallic head with two distinct ends. One side of the head is a flat, broad blade suitable for chopping, while the opposite side tapers to a pointed pick, ideal for breaking or prying. The handle is cylindrical, slightly contoured for grip, and capped at the end, likely for added durability and comfort. The metal head is securely mounted onto the handle, with visible fastenings and a matte, slightly worn finish. The tool exhibits a utilitarian design, with no visible decorative elements, focusing on function and durability. Its size and construction suggest it is intended for manual use in tasks such as splitting, breaking, or prying materials commonly encountered in urban maintenance or construction.
description: A sturdy hand axe with a dark wooden handle and a dual-ended metal head, featuring a flat blade and a pointed pick. The design is utilitarian, emphasizing durability and function.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden handle and both the flat blade and pointed pick ends of the metal head.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the narrow profile of the blade and pick, as well as the rounded handle grip.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again displaying the handle and both ends of the head, with the pick and blade reversed from view 0.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view focusing on the handle's end and the alignment of the head, showing the thickness and attachment of the metal head.
attribute_handle_type: Cylindrical, straight wooden handle with a slight ergonomic contour and a rounded end cap for grip.
attribute_blade_design: Dual-ended head with one flat, rectangular blade and one pointed pick; no beard, with a utilitarian, functional profile.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the metal head fitted onto the wooden handle.
